title: Immunocytochemistry of human iPSC-derived Dopamine Neurons (DaNs)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1r2dj","text":"This protocol outlines the immunocytochemical staining of multiple protein targets for characterisation and synapses in Paraformaldehyde (PFA)-fixed cells plated in a 96-well format plate.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2j8s6","text":"Cells should be plated at a density of 50 000 cells/well in a 96 area full area plate which has been precoated with Geltrex.\n","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a24v","text":"Additionally, care should be taken when adding and removing liquid as these adherent cells are prone to peeling. If the plates are stored prior to staining, a plastic strip should be used to seal the wells to prevent evaporation or contamination, and the plates should be kept at 4°C.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":159,"length":13},{"style":"bold","offset":280,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"a7oka","text":"Cells must be fixed with PFA under a fume hood. Any materials (e.g., pipette tips, reservoirs, or waste containers) that come in contact with PFA should remain inside the fume hood for a minimum of 24 hours before discarding. PFA waste should be discarded as per local guidelines.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":63,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":279,"length":1},{"style":"bold","offset":171,"length":10},{"style":"bold","offset":198,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Remove the media from the wells to be fixed.
Add 50ul of 4% PFA solution to the wells and keep it for 5 minutes for synaptic markers and incubate for 5 minutes for synaptic markers or 10 minutes for other markers at room temperature (RT).
Remove PFA and wash the cells twice with PBS.
Permeabilise the cells with 0.01% Triton X-100 for 1 hour.
Remove the solution and wash with PBS.
Prepare the primary antibody solution in 1% NS in PBS, supplemented with the appropriate primary antibodies (see Materials ) at their respective working concentrations as in Table 1 . %s %s
Add 50 µl of primary antibody solution to each well and incubate overnight at 4°C.
Remove the primary antibody solution and wash twice with PBS.
Incubate in species-appropriate Alexa Fluor® secondary antibodies as mentioned in Table 2 with DAPI (1:10000) in 1% NS in PBS for 1 hour at room temperature. %s %s

Acquire wells images using a 40x magnification water immersion objective Opera Phenix High content screening system, capturing images at a focus level of +1 µm and a binning factor of 1 for synaptic imaging.
For characterisation, acquire images using 40x magnification water immersion objective, capturing images at five focal planes ranging from +1 µm to +9 µm and a binning factor of 2.
Take 20 images on average per well, with 3-5 wells analysed per cell line per differentiation.
